response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer with deep systems and web experience, but the profile does not match this role's mandatory requirements. His C++ experience is limited to a short stint at National Instruments (~6 months in 2014–2015) and a thesis project, far from the required 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development. There is no evidence of SQL Server or Oracle depth (his database work is MongoDB/MySQL), and no Fixed Income, derivatives, or capital-markets domain exposure — RIDE Capital was a retail investment product using Node/TypeScript, not investment banking. The gap on all mandatory criteria makes him unsuitable despite solid engineering fundamentals.
strengths: Strong low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (DLang microservices handling millions of requests/sec)
Solid engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, testing tooling authorship
Broad polyglot background with proven ability to pick up new stacks quickly
concerns: Minimal professional C++ experience (~6 months, a decade ago) versus the mandatory 5–10 years on Windows
No Fixed Income, derivatives, or capital-markets/investment-banking domain experience
No demonstrated SQL Server or Oracle experience; recent stack is TypeScript/Node/MongoDB

key_factors.factor: C++ on Windows requirement not met
Strong systems programming and engineering fundamentals
